column1: C[Phelan: Heh, thanks for the game
saisaysplaygo: definitely!  what do you think my rating should be?
Phelan: 20k or higher, at least. Hard to tell from just one game. :)
saisaysplaygo: I don't understand how a 10k difference translates to a 9 stone handicap on 9x9
robb.effinger: Well, it seems hard to fit a 10th handicap stone on here, so looks like they cap it at 9?
robb.effinger: Taking suggestions for my opening move... :)
C[saisaysplaygo: I meant that a 10k difference on 9x9 should be 3 or 4 stones

robb.effinger: On the 19x19, against a 1-2 stone handicap, I just kinda play like normal, and assume that my opponent will make a mistake or two to make up the points they started with.. at least, when I have the extra stones, I normally make at least one 10-20 point mistake

Phelan: Sorry people. To speed up the tournament, I'm adjudicating these games.9 stones in a 9x9 is huge! robb actually did much better than me. There was a possibility that he could make one eye. :p

C[saisaysplaygo: yeah, I can't imagine how to do that yet.  I suppose it's mostly about counting points and taking advantage of corner joseki (which I don't know)
Phelan: It has a lot to do with being flexible. Possibly leaving positions unsettled that you'd settle in an even game.
Phelan: also it has a lot to do with taking advantage of mistakes by black that accumulate to even out in points.
Phelan: Well, at least, that's how it should be
Phelan: some players do overplays that black doesn't know how to punish
Phelan: but would get them dead in a more even match
Phelan: I try to avoid that
Phelan: So at least I don't do it on purpose :p
C[saisaysplaygo: haha.  you try not to take advantage of your opponent?
C[Phelan: no, that's not it. I try not to take unfair advantage of my opponent, if it's a handicap game
Phelan: two reasons: I don't think I'm teaching good habits by playing a lot of overplays and generally relying on an weaker opponent's lesser knowledge or reading ability
Phelan: It also reinforces bad habits in my own play, if I do it.
Phelan: if I play something often in handicap games, and it works, I might start playing it in even games despite it being a mistake
C[Phelan: if it was a handicap game in a tournament, well, in that case things change. :p
C[Phelan: I really like J5. good potential :)
C[saisaysplaygo: thank you, I was thinking a jump toward the center was standard in this situation
Phelan: Yeah, but this one has more than just jumping towards the center
C[Phelan: it uses the potential of F5, which is in a weak position
Phelan: I think without F5, a two space jump might be better
C[saisaysplaygo: F5 was placed to threaten cutting your groups and create some forcing moves otherwise.  oh, interesting.  play lightly, right?
C[Phelan: surrounded by two solid (relatively) white groups, it's better to play lightly
saisaysplaygo: I need to improve my life and death game - recommendations?

Phelan: unless that's really boring for you

C[Phelan: by being faster, you get to see the result of plays faster
